title: Quota and liveness observed by a deterministic detector + external token-free monitor; quota state is advisory, never a claim gate
context: Plan 2 review of the status digest's quota seam surfaced two failures in the obvious design (the worker session writes quota.json when it sees a rate-limit). First, the write costs tokens: the act of recording is model inference, and under a HARD usage cap the throttled session may be unable to run inference at all — the component meant to report the outage is the one that is down (chicken-and-egg). Second, the subscription budget is SHARED (HDR-009), so a cap cascades to the entire fleet simultaneously; if every worker is the reporter and every worker is throttled, the human is never told. Verified 2026-06-15: Anthropic exposes NO API for subscription (Pro/Max / Claude Code) 5-hour-rolling-window or weekly usage — the Usage/Cost and Rate-Limits Admin APIs cover org API billing only. So there is no proactive number to query token-free.

options.name: worker-session-self-reports
options.rationale: Simplest: the loop that sees the 429 writes quota.json and fires the notification.
options.tradeoff: Recording is token-dependent and dies with the session under a hard cap; the reporter is the thing that is down. The fleet cascade means no worker can report. Chicken-and-egg.

options.name: engine-tracks-quota
options.rationale: Put quota detection in the sb engine so it is deterministic and unit-tested.
options.tradeoff: Violates the engine's defining invariant (no model calls, no judgment). The engine never dispatches, so it cannot observe a rate-limit signal. Impossible by construction.

options.name: deterministic-detector-plus-external-monitor
options.rationale: Detection is a deterministic PostToolUse hook that catches rate-limit/429 signals on a failed dispatch — token-free, fires even when the session has no tokens left to reason. Surfacing is an external, token-free monitor (a cron'd `sb status --emit` / `sb notify` that only reads .switchboard/ and makes NO model calls), so liveness and quota reach the human even when the entire fleet is capped or dead. quota.json is a status hint consumed read-only by the digest; the engine never gates a claim on it.
options.tradeoff: Adds a second always-on process (the monitor) the operator must keep running; the quota signal is reactive (post-429) and coarse — there is no proactive percentage without unofficial OTEL math against published limits.

steelman.strongest_case: Zero new components: the loop already catches the 429 to back off (spec §8), so writing one file there is nearly free and needs no separate monitor process to babysit.
One place, fully deterministic and unit-tested, with no extra process and no hook fragility — if the engine could see dispatch outcomes it would be the cleanest home.

reasoning: The reporter must not be the thing that fails. A token-free deterministic hook detects the rate-limit signal regardless of remaining quota, and a token-free external monitor (pure `sb` CLI over local files, no Anthropic auth, no model calls) delivers the signal independently of fleet health — the same external-observer answer that also covers silent session death (spec §11 #3), so one process solves both. This is consistent with PHI-001 (background services must be session-independent) without its auth hazard, because the monitor makes no model/API calls at all — it only reads .switchboard/, sidestepping the nested-auth/contention failure that burned the hindsight-embed daemon. The hard invariant: quota stays ADVISORY. A shared throttle is simultaneous across the fleet (HDR-009); a quota-gated `sb claim` plus a stale quota.json would lock every worker out and keep them locked after the window reset. quota.json informs humans and tunes backoff; it never blocks the queue.
blast_radius: Orchestration + observability layer only; no sb engine code change. Shapes Plan 2 (the digest reads quota.json read-only, defaulting to ok; documented as advisory) and Plan 3 (the rate-limit detection hook, the worker backoff that writes quota.json, and the external cron monitor running `sb status --emit`/`sb notify`). Reversal is cheap: deleting quota.json degrades the system to no-quota-signal, never to incorrect gating.
